Table rules: Remove all invalid using directives that reference non‑existent namespaces (e.g., Aspose.Words.Tables.BorderType).
Import only the required namespaces: Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing, Aspose.Words.Tables, and System.Drawing.
Clear the default paragraph in the cell with `cell.RemoveAllChildren();` before adding custom content.
Create and populate the paragraph/run after clearing the cell.
Loading rules: Optionally check that the template file exists before loading to avoid runtime errors.
Saving/Export rules: Use TiffSaveOptions (derived from ImageSaveOptions) when saving to TIFF; ImageSaveOptions does not expose MultiPageLayout.
Set the MultiPageLayout property on TiffSaveOptions, not on ImageSaveOptions.
Use Aspose.Words.Saving.TiffSaveOptions for TIFF output instead of ImageSaveOptions.MultiPageLayout, which no longer exists.
Add required using directives (Aspose.Words.Saving) for the save‑options class.
Save the final document with Document.Save().
Save the output to a valid path and optionally inform the user via Console.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Keep the using directives for System.Drawing, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing, and Aspose.Words.Saving.
Insert the group shape with DocumentBuilder.InsertGroupShape(params ShapeBase[] shapes) – the method automatically calculates the group’s bounds.
ShapeBase.ShapeType is read‑only; you cannot assign a new value to it after the shape is created.
To create a shape of a different type, instantiate a new Shape with the required ShapeType (e.g., new Shape(doc, ShapeType.Triangle)).
When you need to add an additional shape to an existing GroupShape, use GroupShape.AppendChild(shape) instead of calling InsertGroupShape again.
InsertGroupShape calculates the group’s bounds based on the shapes passed to it; pass all shapes you want to be grouped at once or add later via AppendChild.
Retrieve a SmartArt shape from a template using GetChild(NodeType.Shape, index, true).
Verify that the retrieved shape is of type ShapeType.SmartArt before proceeding.
Call Shape.UpdateSmartArtDrawing() to ensure the SmartArt rendering is up‑to‑date.
Import the shape into the target document with Document.ImportNode(..., ImportFormatMode.KeepSourceFormatting).
Insert the imported shape into the document using DocumentBuilder.InsertNode().
No additional changes are required; the shape is already inserted into the document by InsertShape.
Add required using directives: System, System.IO, System.Linq, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing.
Reference the Aspose.Words for .NET NuGet package (or DLL) in the project file to resolve Document, DocumentBuilder, Shape, NodeType, etc.
Use the fully‑qualified NodeType enum from Aspose.Words (NodeType.Shape).
Use a ShapeType that starts with "Text" (e.g., ShapeType.TextPlainText) so the shape is recognised as WordArt.
Set the shape's FillColor and StrokeColor directly on the Shape object, not on its TextPath.
Configure WordArt‑specific formatting via the Shape.TextPath property (Bold, Italic, Size, TextPathAlignment, etc.).
Append the shape to a paragraph (inline) or use DocumentBuilder for floating shapes.
Use a shape type that exists in the referenced Aspose.Words version (ShapeType.Group) as a container for child shapes.
Append child shapes to the container using canvas.AppendChild(childShape).
Use GroupShape to represent a drawing canvas; there is no ShapeType.DrawingCanvas enum.
Save with OoxmlSaveOptions.Compliance = Iso29500_2008_Transitional to keep DML shapes.
Insert the GroupShape into the document with DocumentBuilder.InsertNode before adding child shapes.
Set Width/Height (and optionally position) on the GroupShape so child shapes fit inside.
Append child Shape objects to the GroupShape via AppendChild.
Do not use a non‑existent 'Text' property on a Shape of type TextBox; add text by inserting a Paragraph/Run into the shape's child nodes.
Create and configure additional shapes, then add them to the GroupShape with AppendChild.
Adjust the group's internal coordinate system (CoordSize, CoordOrigin) after all child shapes have been added if needed.
Keep required using directives for System, System.Drawing, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing, and Aspose.Words.Saving.
Do not change the core logic that creates shapes, groups them, and saves to XPS.
Charts rules: Chart.Series.Add expects a double[] for the values parameter; use double literals or cast the array to double[].
Keep all required Aspose.Words namespaces (Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing, Aspose.Words.Drawing.Charts).
